city_personality.slang_reaction: Chyort vozmi, Moscow is far but the winter is already here.

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the Edge of the Empire, Mayor
opening_sequence.intro: Moscow is seven time zones away and only remembers you when they need the Pacific Fleet. Sanctions have strangled the commercial port, oligarchs control the smuggling routes, and the brutal winter threatens to freeze the crumbling Soviet-era heating pipes solid.

advisors.name: Dmitry Volkov
advisors.hidden_agenda: Siphoning municipal customs revenue into a slush fund for a local oligarch.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Implementing an independent digital customs tracking system to stop smuggling.

briefing: Moscow expects their cut of the port tariffs by Friday.
We have a 'budget shortfall', but I can find the money if we don't ask questions.
The black market is the only market keeping this city afloat right now.
agreement: A pragmatic choice. The money will flow.
The right people will remember this favor.
disagreement: You are choking the lifeblood of our economy.
Moscow won't save you when the treasury runs dry.
dialogue.betrayal: You cut off my partners. I've sent the real ledgers to the FSB. Enjoy your cell.
roast: You count rubles like a peasant hoarding potatoes.
I've seen drunken sailors with better financial foresight.
scandal_reaction: Deny everything and blame Western sanctions.
I am routing the funds through Harbin as we speak.

advisors.name: Elena Rostova
advisors.hidden_agenda: Legalizing and heavily taxing the grey-market Asian imports to build a legitimate logistics hub, alienating the old oligarchs.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Re-nationalizing the Special Economic Zone or giving monopoly customs rights back to the FSB.

briefing: The Chinese logistics firms are offering to build a free-trade warehouse district, legally.
We are losing billions in unrecorded cross-border e-commerce.
Moscow wants 'import substitution', but we need 'export facilitation' to survive.
agreement: A rational step toward a modern economy.
The Asian partners will appreciate this clarity.
disagreement: You are letting 1990s racketeers dictate 21st-century trade policy.
This will drive the legitimate investors straight to our competitors.
dialogue.betrayal: You gave the customs monopoly back to the siloviki. I am taking my trade data to the Ministry of Economic Development.
roast: You run a port city like a closed Soviet factory.
Your economic vision ends at the end of the Trans-Siberian railway.
scandal_reaction: I will publish the open-ledger trade metrics to prove our innocence.
This is just the oligarchs trying to protect their smuggling routes.

advisors.name: Admiral Igor Ivanov
advisors.hidden_agenda: Expanding the naval base exclusion zone into the lucrative civilian commercial ports.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Denying the navy expansion to keep the commercial docks open to foreign trade.

briefing: The Pacific Fleet requires priority access to the eastern docks today.
We have detected foreign surveillance vessels near Russky Island.
Civilian concerns are secondary to the security of the Russian Federation.
agreement: A strong decision for the Motherland.
The Fleet acknowledges your cooperation.
disagreement: You are compromising national security for petty commerce.
Do not forget who truly controls this peninsula.
dialogue.betrayal: You denied the Fleet. The military is taking over the port jurisdiction effective immediately.
roast: You govern like a cowardly merchant, not a leader of Russia.
You would sell the nuclear codes for a shipment of Japanese cars.
scandal_reaction: The military will not be dragged into your civilian filth.
Secure the naval bases. Let the city burn.

advisors.name: Colonel Viktor Kuznetsov
advisors.hidden_agenda: Establishing a massive digital surveillance and informant network among migrant workers to root out 'foreign espionage', chilling legitimate trade.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Signing a visa-free travel agreement with neighboring Asian countries or defunding the FSB cyber-surveillance center.

briefing: We have intercepted three encrypted communications from the foreign student dormitories.
The border guards need more drones. The taiga is vast and unwatched.
Every new foreign business is a potential intelligence vector.
agreement: Vigilance is the price of sovereignty.
The Directorate appreciates your awareness of the threat.
disagreement: You are blinding us to protect the profits of smugglers.
Naivety is a luxury the Russian Federation cannot afford.
dialogue.betrayal: You opened the borders. I have classified your administration as a compromised asset. Pack your bags.
roast: You trust foreigners more than your own security services.
Your open-door policy is an invitation for sabotage.
scandal_reaction: The FSB will conduct a 'routine' audit of your office.
I suggest you cooperate fully with the investigators. For your health.

advisors.name: Svetlana Morozova
advisors.hidden_agenda: Modernizing the crumbling Soviet-era central heating grid before winter, regardless of cost.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Diverting the winter heating funds to build a vanity project for Moscow officials.

briefing: The central heating pipes in the Pervomaysky district are rusting through.
Winter is coming, Mayor. We don't have enough coal reserves.
The Soviet-era apartment blocs are literally crumbling into the sea.
agreement: Finally, we are looking after our own people.
The residents will not freeze this winter.
disagreement: People will die of hypothermia because of this.
You care more about appearances than basic survival.
dialogue.betrayal: You stole the heating money for a monument. I'm telling the press, and I hope you freeze.
roast: Your legacy will be a city of ice and concrete ruins.
You are as hollow as our rusted water pipes.
scandal_reaction: This administration is rotting from the inside out.
I will not go to prison for your greed.

advisors.name: Arkady Lebedev
advisors.hidden_agenda: Demolishing the historic and Soviet-era city center to build a massive, debt-funded 'Asian Gateway' mega-development zone with his cronies.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Enacting strict heritage protection laws or freezing all new commercial construction permits to assess structural safety.

briefing: The old shipyard is prime real estate. We can have it cleared by spring.
Beijing investors want to build a 50-story hotel. They just need the zoning changed.
Those crumbling Khrushchyovkas are an eyesore. Let the wrecking balls swing.
agreement: Progress requires breaking a few eggs, Mayor.
The cranes will be a beautiful sight on the skyline.
disagreement: You are holding this city hostage to its own decay.
We cannot build a global city on a foundation of rotting concrete.
dialogue.betrayal: You froze the permits. The investors are pulling out. You've condemned this city to remain a provincial backwater.
roast: You love the rust so much you want to live in it.
Your urban plan is just a museum of Soviet failures.
scandal_reaction: The environmental assessments were completely legal. Mostly.
Blame the previous administration for the structural flaws.

advisors.name: Yuri Smirnov
advisors.hidden_agenda: Protecting the grey-market imported Japanese car smuggling ring at the docks.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Inviting federal anti-corruption police to inspect the cargo terminals.

briefing: The Golden Horn Bay is choked with delayed cargo ships.
Moscow is complaining about the right-hand drive cars again.
The dockworkers union is threatening a strike over unpaid wages.
agreement: The cargo keeps moving, boss.
My guys at the port will appreciate this.
disagreement: You're jamming up the whole supply chain.
Don't blame me when the goods stop flowing.
dialogue.betrayal: You brought the Feds to my docks. You're dead to the union, and maybe just dead.
roast: You couldn't navigate a puddle, let alone a seaport.
This city is stalling out under your watch.
scandal_reaction: I'm wiping the cargo manifests right now.
Time to take a long vacation to Harbin.

advisors.name: Oksana Tarasova
advisors.hidden_agenda: Diverting all municipal transport funds to subsidize the federally-owned Trans-Siberian railway and its corrupt freight monopolies, starving local roads.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Investing municipal funds into local highway repairs or independent regional trucking cooperatives instead of the federal railway monopoly.

briefing: The Trans-Siberian freight volumes are up, but the federal monopoly demands higher municipal track subsidies.
The local roads are full of potholes, but the rail spur to the port needs electrification.
Moscow is threatening to reroute the container traffic to Nakhodka if we don't pay up.
agreement: The railway is the spine of Russia. You have supported the spine.
Federal Moscow will note your compliance.
disagreement: You are starving the federal infrastructure to pave local dirt roads.
Without the rail monopoly, this port ceases to exist.
dialogue.betrayal: You defunded the rail spur. I will ensure Moscow downgrades this port's federal status. Good luck with your potholes.
roast: You think a paved road matters when the trains stop running?
You manage logistics like a blind man feeling a map.
scandal_reaction: The federal subsidies were strictly allocated according to Moscow's directives.
I will have the railway lawyers tie this up in arbitration for a decade.

advisors.name: Father Nikolai
advisors.hidden_agenda: Building a massive, gold-domed Orthodox cathedral on prime commercial waterfront real estate.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Selling the waterfront cathedral plot to Chinese casino developers.

briefing: The moral decay of this port city is a stain on the Motherland.
The Patriarch in Moscow is watching our spiritual progress.
We must build a beacon of faith overlooking the Pacific.
agreement: God blesses this righteous decision.
The bells will ring in your honor.
disagreement: You are choosing Mammon over salvation.
The church will not forget this slight.
dialogue.betrayal: You sold holy ground to foreign gamblers. I have excommunicated you and your administration.
roast: Your soul is darker than a Siberian winter night.
Even a Soviet commissar had more spiritual depth than you.
scandal_reaction: The wages of sin are now public.
I will pray for your redemption, though I doubt it will come.

advisors.name: Director Timur Yusupov
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing exclusive municipal contracts for Islamic and Buddhist cultural centers by playing the federal 'traditional values' card, while skimming construction budgets.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Promoting a strictly Russian Orthodox-only civic identity or defunding the 'Traditional Asian Faiths' municipal grant program.

briefing: The Central Asian migrant workforce needs a recognized mosque, or we risk radicalization in the shadows.
The local Buddhist community is requesting land for a new datsan. It aligns with Moscow's 'Eastern pivot'.
Father Nikolai is overstepping. The Far East is not just Orthodox.
agreement: A harmonious balance for our multi-ethnic reality.
The community leaders will be very grateful for this state support.
disagreement: You are alienating the very demographics that keep our economy running.
Catering only to the Orthodox Church is a demographic dead end.
dialogue.betrayal: You gave the Church a monopoly on civic morality. I am mobilizing the ethnic business blocs to freeze your municipal contracts.
roast: You think this is Moscow? We are in Asia, Mayor. Act like it.
Your piety is as fake as the gold leaf on Nikolai's dome.
scandal_reaction: The grant money was distributed perfectly according to inter-ethnic quotas.
This is just the Orthodox lobby trying to eliminate their competition.

decisions.title: Moscow's Car Ban
decisions.body: Federal authorities in Moscow have mandated a strict ban on right-hand drive Japanese cars to support domestic manufacturers. However, 80% of Vladivostok's population drives these cheap, reliable imports.

options.label: Formally request a delayed rollout from Moscow to appease locals.

consequences.public_reaction: Kicking the can down the road, typical bureaucrat.

options.label: Enforce the federal ban immediately, deploying police to impound cars.

consequences.public_reaction: They want us to drive Ladas?! We will burn the police stations!

options.label: Ignore Moscow entirely and declare Vladivostok a 'Special Automotive Zone'.

consequences.public_reaction: The Mayor stood up to Moscow! Long live the Japanese imports!

decisions.title: The Oriental Vegas
decisions.body: A consortium of Chinese investors wants to build a massive, neon-drenched casino resort in the special economic zone. It promises huge tax revenues, but locals fear an influx of organized crime and being priced out of their own city.

options.label: Reject the casino to protect local businesses and morality.

consequences.public_reaction: Glad we aren't selling our soul to foreign gamblers.

options.label: Approve the casino construction for the massive cash injection.

consequences.public_reaction: More money for the oligarchs, more crime for us.

options.label: Approve it, but mandate that Russian citizens are legally barred from gambling there.

consequences.public_reaction: So they build a palace in our city and tell us we can't go inside? Classic.

decisions.title: The Freezing Pipes
decisions.body: The central heating network is a rusty Soviet relic. With winter temperatures dropping to -20°C, engineers warn of a catastrophic system failure if immediate, costly repairs aren't made.

options.label: Patch the worst leaks cheaply and hope it holds until spring.

consequences.public_reaction: We are sleeping in our winter coats again.

options.label: Tear up the main roads to completely replace the core pipeline.

consequences.public_reaction: Traffic is a nightmare, but at least I can feel my toes.

options.label: Abandon the central grid and force residents to buy their own electric heaters.

consequences.public_reaction: The power grid will collapse! You are sentencing us to freeze!

decisions.title: Pacific Fleet Expansion
decisions.body: The Admiral demands the city turn over Pier 4—a vital commercial cargo dock—to the Pacific Fleet for a new nuclear submarine berth. The dockworkers union is furious.

options.label: Yield the pier to the Navy out of patriotic duty.

consequences.public_reaction: More guns, fewer jobs. The Moscow way.

options.label: Protest to Moscow to protect the commercial interests of the port.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, someone standing up to the brass hats.

options.label: Secretly sabotage the pier's foundations so neither can use it.

consequences.public_reaction: A mysterious structural failure? How very convenient.

decisions.title: The Contraband Cargo
decisions.body: Customs has intercepted a massive shipment of sanctioned European luxury goods—Rolex watches, French wine, designer clothing. Moscow hasn't noticed yet.

options.label: Quietly auction it off to local elites to fill the city budget.

consequences.public_reaction: The rich drink champagne while we eat cabbage.

options.label: Seize and publicly destroy the goods with bulldozers as a show of patriotism.

consequences.public_reaction: A grand spectacle, but a tragic waste of good wine.

options.label: Distribute the luxury goods to the angry dockworkers as 'bonuses'.

consequences.public_reaction: I traded my new Gucci bag for firewood! Best Mayor ever!

crises.name: The Great Freeze
crises.trigger: A massive blizzard has knocked out the power grid, and the central heating plant has critically failed.
crises.description: Temperatures are plummeting to -25°C. Hundreds of thousands of residents are trapped in freezing concrete apartment blocks without heat or electricity. Pipes are bursting across the city. People will start dying of hypothermia by nightfall.

options.label: Beg the Pacific Fleet to deploy emergency portable generators and fuel.
Divert all municipal funds immediately to emergency cash payments for repairs.
Blame Moscow for the infrastructure failure and encourage citizens to loot for firewood.

crises.name: Federal Anti-Corruption Raid
crises.trigger: FSB agents from Moscow have arrived unannounced to investigate hundreds of millions in missing port revenues.
crises.description: They are raiding City Hall and seizing hard drives. The Kremlin wants a scapegoat for the rampant smuggling and tax evasion. If you don't give them a target, they will arrest you and dissolve the local government.

options.label: Throw the Transport Advisor under the bus and hand over doctored ledgers.
Refuse to cooperate, claiming local jurisdiction, and rally the local oligarchs.
Bribe the lead FSB investigator with a permanent cut of the smuggling ring.

crises.name: Global Sanctions Chokehold
crises.trigger: New international sanctions have completely frozen the port's remaining legal shipping operations.
crises.description: Foreign vessels refuse to dock. The cranes are idle. Thousands of dockworkers are suddenly unemployed, and the city's primary revenue stream has evaporated overnight. Economic collapse is days away.

options.label: Institute severe austerity measures, cutting municipal salaries across the board.
Fully transition the port to overt black-market operations with North Korea and China.
Print local 'Far East' debt bonds and violently force local businesses to buy them.

crises.name: The Moscow Ultimatum
crises.trigger: The Kremlin, tired of the city's corruption and independence, issues an ultimatum: step down or face federal charges.
crises.description: The President is preparing to sign a decree replacing you with a federal governor-general from Moscow. OMON riot police are massing at the airport. You have hours to decide the fate of your administration and your freedom.

options.label: Resign peacefully in exchange for immunity and a cushy state corporate job.
Rally the locals and declare Vladivostok an autonomous 'Free Port Republic'.
Flee across the border to China in the dead of night with the city treasury.

scandals.title: Mayor's Son Crashes Smuggled Sports Car
scandals.description: Video has emerged of your son crashing a highly illegal, heavily modified Japanese sports car into a monument while street racing.

scandals.title: Heating Funds Found in Cyprus
scandals.description: Investigative journalists have traced millions of rubles meant for winter pipe repairs directly to offshore accounts controlled by your associates.

scandals.title: Mayor Caught Selling Pacific Fleet Movements
scandals.description: The FSB has released a damning audio recording of you offering to sell confidential access schedules for the naval shipyards to foreign intelligence agents.

positive: Finally, a Mayor who understands how things actually work here.
Moscow is far, but at least someone is looking out for us.
The heating works today. A miracle.
As long as the port stays open, I support him.
He might be corrupt, but he's *our* corrupt Mayor.
Good. Let Moscow choke on their own rules.
neutral: Another day, another bribe. Nothing changes in the East.
I'm just trying to keep my Lada running.
Winter is coming. The rest is just noise.
They pretend to pay us, we pretend to work.
Bozhe moy, more drama from the Duma.
I'll believe it when I see the potholes fixed.
negative: Thieves. Every single one of them in City Hall.
My apartment is freezing and they are buying yachts!
They sold this city to the highest bidder years ago.
Moscow needs to send the FSB to arrest the whole building.
We are suffocating under sanctions and corruption.
Resign before the people freeze to death!
media: Vesti Primorye: Local officials announce new port regulations.
PrimaMedia: Heating pipe bursts in Pervomaysky, hundreds freezing.
Kommersant: Moscow expresses 'deep concern' over Vladivostok leadership.
Local Telegram: The Japanese car market is panicking over new rumors.
VladNews: Pacific Fleet conducts unexpected drills near commercial docks.
Breaking: Federal investigators spotted at Vladivostok airport.
politician: We must prioritize the stability of the Russian Federation.
The Mayor's actions border on insubordination.
We demand an immediate audit of the municipal budget.
This is a betrayal of the working class of the Far East.
The West is trying to divide us; we must stand strong.
I distance myself entirely from this administration's choices.
activist: Fix the pipes! We are freezing!
Stop selling our waterfront to oligarchs and casinos!
Where did the municipal funds go? Show us the ledgers!
We demand clean air, not more coal dust from the port!
Save our right-hand drive cars! It's our culture!
End the corruption before the city collapses entirely!
street: Blyad, my engine won't start, it's too cold.
Did you hear the price of fish went up again?
The customs guys are asking for double the bribe today.
I had to sleep in my winter coat again last night.
Just got a sweet deal on some smuggled tires.
Moscow doesn't care if we fall into the ocean.
crisis: We are freezing to death! Help us!
The lights are out! Everything is dark!
The Feds are here! Hide the paperwork!
The port is dead. We are all going to starve.
The riot police are on the streets! Run!
This is the end of Vladivostok.
scandal: Arrest him! Throw him in the freezing sea!
Shameless thieves. They have no conscience.
Selling our secrets? He is a traitor to the Motherland!
I knew it. They are all rats feeding on the city.
Send him to a penal colony in Siberia!
Disgusting. We suffer while they steal millions.

scandal_reactions.minor: Like we didn't already know they think they own the city.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Disgraceful. We expect them to steal, but not this openly.
scandal_reactions.major: People are freezing while you hide money in Cyprus! Hang him!
scandal_reactions.career_ending: Treason against the Motherland. The FSB will handle him.

romance_exposure.flavour: In the cynical, hard-edged culture of the Far East, a personal affair is barely news unless municipal funds were used to pay for the mistress's apartment.
